backlog.title: Stop stripping #@ markers; preserve the prompt verbatim
backlog.description: Plan 008 ships a working extension whose input handler strips #@ from the user prompt and strips resolved #@/bare-@ markers from delivered markdown content before storing the message. This phase reverses that: the prompt is never modified. The input handler returns event.text byte-for-byte intact; delivered markdown files keep their import markers verbatim. Root cause (verified in agent-session.ts): Pi re-feeds the STORED user-message content on cancel/fork/tree-navigate — if #@ was stripped from the stored message, re-submission finds no trigger and files silently vanish. Preserving verbatim makes re-submission reliably re-trigger injection. See architecture/system_context.md and architecture/stripping_logic_analysis.md for verified line numbers.

milestones.title: Remove stripping from the injection engine and input handler (file-injector.ts)
milestones.description: Remove the two stripping sites (top-level prompt in injectFiles, markdown content in injectMarkdown), simplify scanTokens to return string[] and processTokenStream to return void, and make the input handler return event.text verbatim. All changes are in file-injector.ts. The subtasks within T1 are tightly coupled by the scanTokens return-type cascade and MUST be implemented atomically in one pass (S1->S2->S3) for the file to remain type-consistent. T2 follows to update the input handler return and verify the delivery pipeline is unaffected.

tasks.title: Engine simplification: scanTokens->string[], processTokenStream->void, injectMarkdown verbatim, injectFiles verbatim
tasks.description: Remove all stripping logic from the injection engine. This is one atomic refactor across four functions because the scanTokens return-type change cascades to every consumer. The three subtasks (S1, S2, S3) are implemented in sequence within a single pass — the file will not type-check until all three are done. After this task: scanTokens returns Promise<string[]> (resolved abs paths), processTokenStream returns Promise<void>, injectMarkdown emits verbatim content (Steps 3.5+4 deleted), injectFiles returns the original prompt text verbatim.

subtasks.title: scanTokens -> Promise<string[]>; processTokenStream -> Promise<void>
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: scanTokens is at file-injector.ts L856-893 with JSDoc at L826-854. It currently returns Promise<{ index: number; prefixLen: number; abs: string }[]>. The cands array (L873) holds { idx, token, prefixLen } and the output (L886) pushes { index: c.idx, prefixLen: c.prefixLen, abs }. The index/prefixLen fields exist ONLY to support marker stripping, which is being removed. processTokenStream is at L904-919; it consumes scanTokens' records, calls injectFile per record, and returns Promise<number[]> (the r.index values where injectFile returned true). Its opts.bareAt is typed boolean (required) while scanTokens' is bareAt? (optional) — harmonize both to bareAt?: boolean since the only caller always passes a value.
2. INPUT: Current scanTokens signature (L856-861) and processTokenStream signature (L904-910). The internal cands loop (L873-875) builds { idx, token, prefixLen }[]. The processTokenStream body (L911-918) iterates records, calls injectFile, collects resolved indices.
3. LOGIC:
  scanTokens: Change return type to Promise<string[]>. In the cands array, DROP prefixLen — push { idx: m.index!, token: m[2] } for both FILE_INJECT_RE and BARE_AT_RE matches. In the output, push ONLY abs (string) instead of { index, prefixLen, abs }. The idx field stays in cands (needed for inCode(c.idx, codeRanges) at L882). Change out type from { index; prefixLen; abs }[] to string[]. Rewrite JSDoc (L826-854): remove all 'prefixLen'/'so a consumer can strip' language; document that scanTokens returns resolved abs paths in encounter order for depth-first recursion, and that markers are detected ONLY to resolve imports — never stripped (PRD §6.4, §12.16).
  processTokenStream: Change return type to Promise<void>. Remove the resolved: number[] accumulator (L912). Change `const records = await scanTokens(...)` to `const absPaths = await scanTokens(...)`. Change the loop to `for (const abs of absPaths) { if (state.injectedSet.has(abs)) continue; await injectFile(abs, state, ctx); }`. Remove the `if (ok) resolved.push(r.index)` line. Remove `return resolved`. Rewrite JSDoc (L894-910): document that processTokenStream returns void (no strip indices), scans once before injecting, and each resolved abs is injected depth-first via injectFile.
  CRITICAL CASCADE: Changing scanTokens' return type breaks injectMarkdown (L1105, calls scanTokens expecting records) and injectFiles (L1225, calls processTokenStream expecting number[]). These callers are updated in S2 and S3 respectively. The file will NOT type-check until S2 and S3 are complete — implement all three subtasks in sequence within one pass.
4. OUTPUT: scanTokens exports Promise<string[]> (resolved abs paths, encounter order). processTokenStream is private, returns Promise<void>. Both have updated JSDoc reflecting the verbatim-delivery contract.
5. DOCS: Mode A — rewrite JSDoc on scanTokens and processTokenStream to reflect the new return types and the 'markers detected, never stripped' contract. These are internal-function docs that ride WITH the code change.

subtasks.title: injectMarkdown: delete Step 3.5 + Step 4; emit verbatim content; update recursion
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: injectMarkdown is at file-injector.ts L1094-1159 with JSDoc at L1040-1093. It implements a 6-step algorithm: Step 2 (claim self, L1098), Step 3 (scan for imports, L1105), Step 3.5 (stat/fs.access pre-check building injectable[], L1129-1140), Step 4 (strip markers from content producing `stripped`, L1145-1147), Step 5 (emit block on stripped content, L1151), Step 6 (recurse into injectable[], L1154-1158). Steps 3.5 and 4 exist ONLY to support stripping and must be deleted. After S1, scanTokens returns string[] so `records` at L1105 is now string[] (abs paths).
2. INPUT: The updated scanTokens from P1.M1.T1.S1 (returns Promise<string[]>). The current injectMarkdown at L1094-1159. The `content` parameter (the markdown file's decoded UTF-8) is already available — it does NOT need transformation.
3. LOGIC:
  a) L1105: Change `const records = await scanTokens(content, dir, { allowAbsTilde: false, skipCode: true, tryMdExt: true, bareAt: state.bareAt }, state)` to `const absPaths = await scanTokens(...)`. Since scanTokens now returns string[], absPaths is string[].
  b) DELETE Step 3.5 entirely (L1107-1140): the stat/fs.access pre-check, the injectable[] array, the for-loop iterating records to build injectable. This block existed ONLY to gate stripping — it served no other purpose. The stat/exists check that injectable provided is redundant with injectFile's own stat at L947 (injectFile re-stats harmlessly on recursion).
  c) DELETE Step 4 entirely (L1141-1147): the `let stripped = content` line and the for-loop that slices markers out. The `stripped` variable is gone.
  d) L1151 (Step 5, now renumbered Step 4): Change `emitText(abs, stripped, state)` to `emitText(abs, content, state)`. The paged decision now runs on VERBATIM content (PRD §5.6 step 4, §6.4). The marker text adds a negligible number of chars to the budget estimate.
  e) L1154-1158 (Step 6, now renumbered Step 5): Change `for (const r of injectable)` to `for (const abs2 of absPaths)`. Change `if (state.injectedSet.has(r.abs))` to `if (state.injectedSet.has(abs2))`. Change `await injectFile(r.abs, state, ctx)` to `await injectFile(abs2, state, ctx)`.
  f) Rewrite the JSDoc (L1040-1093): document the now-5-step algorithm (claim self -> scan for imports -> emit verbatim block -> recurse depth-first). Remove all references to Step 3.5, Step 4, 'stripped', 'injectable', 'prefixLen', and the readability pre-check. Add: 'Markers are detected ONLY to resolve imports — they are NEVER stripped from the delivered content (PRD §6.4). The block is emitted on the verbatim content exactly as read from disk.'
4. OUTPUT: injectMarkdown delivers the markdown file's content VERBATIM (markers preserved), still recurses into resolved imports depth-first, still dedups via injectedSet. The function signature is unchanged (async (abs, content, state, ctx) => Promise<void>).
5. DOCS: Mode A — rewrite JSDoc on injectMarkdown to reflect the 5-step algorithm and the 'never stripped' contract.

subtasks.title: injectFiles: delete resolvedIdx/strippedText; return original prompt verbatim
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: injectFiles is at file-injector.ts L1162-1246. At L1225 it calls `const resolvedIdx = await processTokenStream(text, ctx.cwd, { allowAbsTilde: true, skipCode: false, tryMdExt: false, bareAt: false }, state, ctx)`. After S1, processTokenStream returns void (not number[]), so this assignment must be removed. At L1240-1242, `strippedText` is computed by splicing each index in resolvedIdx by +2. At L1246, the function returns `{ text: strippedText, ... }`. The `count === 0` early return at L1228 already returns the original `text` (verbatim) — this path is unchanged. After this subtask, BOTH paths return the original `text`.
2. INPUT: The updated processTokenStream from P1.M1.T1.S1 (returns Promise<void>). The current injectFiles at L1162-1246. The function's first parameter `text: string` is the original prompt — it is returned verbatim after this change.
3. LOGIC:
  a) L1225: Change `const resolvedIdx = await processTokenStream(...)` to just `await processTokenStream(text, ctx.cwd, { allowAbsTilde: true, skipCode: false, tryMdExt: false, bareAt: false }, state, ctx)`. Drop the const assignment (void return).
  b) L1228 (unchanged): The `if (state.count === 0) return { text, images: imagesIn, injected: 0, paged: 0, blocks: [], details: [] }` early return already returns the original `text` — no change needed.
  c) DELETE L1239-1245: The entire `let strippedText = text; for (...) { strippedText = strippedText.slice(...) }` block AND its associated comments. This was the top-level stripping logic.
  d) L1246: Change `return { text: strippedText, images: state.images, injected: state.count, paged: state.paged, blocks: state.blocks, details: state.details }` to `return { text, images: state.images, injected: state.count, paged: state.paged, blocks: state.blocks, details: state.details }`. The `text` here is the function's first parameter (the original, verbatim prompt).
  e) Update any comments near L1225 and L1246 that reference 'stripping', 'resolvedIdx', or 'strippedText' — they must now say the prompt is returned verbatim (PRD §6.4).
4. OUTPUT: injectFiles returns `{ text, images, injected, paged, blocks, details }` where `text` is the ORIGINAL prompt byte-for-byte (unchanged). The blocks and details arrays still contain the <file> blocks and FileDetail metadata for before_agent_start to publish. The return TYPE is unchanged (same shape).
5. DOCS: none — internal function return-value semantics; no user-facing/config/API surface change. The user-facing display description is Mode B (P1.M2.T4).

tasks.title: Input handler returns event.text verbatim; verify delivery/renderer unaffected
tasks.description: The input handler (L1306-1324) currently destructures `text` from injectFiles and returns it as the transform text. After T1, that `text` is already the verbatim prompt (injectFiles no longer strips). This task makes the return explicit (text: event.text) and verifies — read-only — that before_agent_start, computeDetailOffsets, and renderInjectedMessage are unaffected by the verbatim change (they consume blocks/details, never prompt text).

subtasks.title: Input handler: change return to text: event.text (explicit verbatim)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The input handler is at file-injector.ts L1306-1324. At L1310 it calls `const { text, images, injected, paged, blocks, details } = await injectFiles(event.text, event.images ?? [], ctx, cfg.markdownBareAtImports === true)`. After T1, `text` === `event.text` (injectFiles returns verbatim). At L1323 it returns `{ action: "transform" as const, text, images }`. The PRD §6.4 specifies returning `event.text` explicitly for clarity and to match the verbatim contract. The `pending = { blocks, details }` stash at L1317 and the notify at L1320-1321 are unchanged.
2. INPUT: The updated injectFiles from P1.M1.T1.S3 (returns verbatim text as the `text` field). The current input handler at L1306-1324.
3. LOGIC:
  a) L1323: Change `return { action: "transform" as const, text, images }` to `return { action: "transform" as const, text: event.text, images }`. This makes the verbatim contract explicit (PRD §6.4) — even though `text` already equals `event.text` after T1, using `event.text` directly removes any ambiguity about whether the handler modifies the prompt.
  b) Optionally simplify the destructure at L1310: since `text` is no longer needed from injectFiles' return (we use event.text), the destructure can drop `text`. But keeping it is harmless and useful for debugging. Prefer keeping it for now and documenting that `text === event.text` (verbatim).
  c) The `!injected` early return at L1311 (`if (!injected) return { action: "continue" }`) is unchanged — when nothing is injected, the prompt is already byte-for-byte preserved (no transform needed).
  d) The `pending` stash assignment at L1317, the notify at L1320-1321, and all short-circuit guards (L1306-1308) are unchanged.
4. OUTPUT: The input handler returns `{ action: "transform", text: event.text, images }` when files are injected, and `{ action: "continue" }` otherwise. The user message stored by Pi is the prompt EXACTLY as typed — #@ markers preserved (PRD §6.4, §13.8). This makes cancel/fork/tree-navigate re-submission reliably re-trigger injection.
5. DOCS: none — handler internals; the user-facing display description is Mode B (P1.M2.T4).

subtasks.title: Verify before_agent_start, computeDetailOffsets, renderInjectedMessage unaffected (read-only)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Three functions consume the injection pipeline output but do NOT touch the prompt text:
  - before_agent_start handler (L1331-1342): reads `pending = { blocks, details }`, calls computeDetailOffsets(blocks, details), returns `{ message: { customType, content: blocks.join('\n\n'), display: true, details: { files: details } } }`. Consumes ONLY blocks/details.
  - computeDetailOffsets (L353-423): computes absolute char offsets of each file body within blocks.join('\n\n'). Operates on blocks/details ONLY.
  - renderInjectedMessage (L739-825): reads message.details.files (FileDetail[]) and message.content (the joined blocks). Re-parses file bodies from content via FILE_BLOCK_RE. Does NOT touch the user prompt text. Verbatim markers inside a markdown block's body are simply displayed as-is in the expanded view — this is intended honest behavior (matches model input).
2. INPUT: The completed engine changes from P1.M1.T1 (scanTokens->string[], processTokenStream->void, injectMarkdown verbatim, injectFiles verbatim) and the input handler change from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: READ-ONLY verification — do NOT modify these functions. Confirm:
  a) before_agent_start: the `pending` stash is set in the input handler (L1317) and consumed one-shot in before_agent_start (L1333-1341). The stash carries `{ blocks, details }` — these are the <file> blocks and FileDetail metadata, NOT the prompt text. Verbatim markers in markdown blocks are already in `blocks` (emitText pushed the verbatim content). No change needed.
  b) computeDetailOffsets: operates on the `blocks` array (length-derived offsets). Verbatim markers add a few chars to block content — offsets adjust automatically. No change needed.
  c) renderInjectedMessage: re-parses bodies from `message.content` via FILE_BLOCK_RE. A markdown block's body now contains the literal `#@api.md` marker (not stripped) — it renders as text in the expanded view. This is correct and honest (the model sees the same content). No change needed. The renderer's defensive fallback (missing details.files -> single 'read (injected files)' line) is unchanged.
  d) Run `npm run typecheck` (scripts/typecheck.mjs) to confirm the scanTokens/processTokenStream signature changes type-check clean under --strict. This is the gate: if typecheck fails, the cascade was incomplete.
4. OUTPUT: Confirmation (no code changes) that the delivery pipeline and renderer work unchanged with verbatim content. If typecheck passes, M1 is complete.
5. DOCS: none — verification-only subtask.

milestones.title: Migrate tests to verbatim assertions; add re-open regression; sync README
milestones.description: After M1 changes the engine to verbatim delivery, the existing tests (~78 stripping-related assertions across three test files) will FAIL because they assert stripped prompts/markers. This milestone migrates every assertion from 'stripped' to 'verbatim' expectations, adds new re-open/re-submit regression tests (PRD §11 case #42), and syncs the README for the verbatim behavior (Mode B). Tests must all pass via `npm test` (which chains all three test files with &&).

tasks.title: Migrate stripped->verbatim assertions in file-injector.test.mjs
tasks.description: file-injector.test.mjs (2812 lines) is the primary test gate. It currently asserts ~78 stripping-related expectations across three categories: (a) top-level prompt assertions where r.text/out.text was checked for stripped prompts (e.g. r.text === 'Review a.ts'), (b) markdown block content assertions where markers were checked as stripped (hasBlock pairs asserting the marker was removed), and (c) partial-strip / verbatim-prompt assertions. All must flip from 'stripped' to 'verbatim'.

subtasks.title: Migrate top-level prompt assertions (~20): r.text/out.text now verbatim
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_assertions_analysis.md for exact line numbers. The top-level prompt assertions fall into three groups:
  (a) EXACT EQUALITY stripped (4 assertions): L428 (Case 1) `r.text === 'Review a.ts'`; L551 (Case 12) `out.text === 'Review a.ts'`; L2477 (DELIV-1) `r.text === 'Review a.ts'`; L2500 (DELIV-2) `out.text === 'Review a.ts'`. Each must become the VERBATIM form with #@ preserved (e.g. `r.text === 'Review #@a.ts'`).
  (b) STARTS-WITH stripped (14 assertions): L996, L1092, L1638, L1670, L1705, L1785, L1882, L1897, L1915, L1771, L1941, L1954, L1968, L1986. Each asserts `r.text.startsWith('Review a.ts')` (stripped) — must become `r.text.startsWith('Review #@a.ts')` (verbatim, #@ preserved).
  (c) NEGATIVE SHAPE (2 assertions at L2478-2479): `!r.text.includes('---')` and `!r.text.includes('<file')` — these STAY VALID (the verbatim prompt still has no --- separator and no <file> blocks; those live in r.blocks). No change needed.
2. INPUT: The updated engine from P1.M1.T1/P1.M1.T2 that now returns verbatim prompts. The current test assertions at the line numbers above.
3. LOGIC: For EACH of the ~20 top-level assertions:
  - Exact-equality: change the expected string from the stripped form to the verbatim form. E.g. `r.text === 'Review a.ts'` -> `r.text === 'Review #@a.ts'`. Reconstruct the verbatim prompt by re-inserting the exact #@<token> the test's input used. Check the test's setup (the `prompt` variable or literal passed to injectFiles/captureHandler) to get the exact original text.
  - Starts-with: change the prefix from the stripped form to the verbatim form. E.g. `r.text.startsWith('Review a.ts')` -> `r.text.startsWith('Review #@a.ts')`.
  - Update any assertion COMMENT that says 'stripped' or 'marker removed' to say 'verbatim' or '#@ preserved' (PRD §6.4).
  - The `!r.text.includes('---')` and `!r.text.includes('<file')` negative assertions at L2478-2479 are UNCHANGED — they remain valid (verbatim prompt has no blocks or separator).
  - IMPORTANT: the partial-strip assertions (FS1/FS2/FS3/F2 at L899-L933) are handled in S3, not here.
4. OUTPUT: All ~20 top-level prompt assertions in file-injector.test.mjs now expect VERBATIM prompts (#@ preserved). The test file runs without assertion failures on these cases.
5. DOCS: none — test file.

subtasks.title: Migrate markdown block content assertions (~13 pairs): markers now present verbatim
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_assertions_analysis.md for exact line numbers. There are ~13 stripped-marker PAIRS (26 individual assertions) in file-injector.test.mjs where a markdown block's content was checked for STRIPPED markers. Each pair has a positive assertion (the stripped form is present) and a negative assertion (the #@ form is absent). After verbatim delivery, these FLIP: the #@ form is now PRESENT and the stripped-only form is absent (or both are present since the path text remains). Key examples:
  - L1644-1645 (Case 15): `hasBlock(r, 'Imports api.md here.')` + `!hasBlock(r, 'Imports #@api.md here.')` -> flip to `hasBlock(r, 'Imports #@api.md here.')` (marker present).
  - L1752-1753 (Case 20): `r.blocks[iB].includes('Logs: huge.log')` + `!r.blocks[iB].includes('#@')` -> the `!includes('#@')` must be REMOVED or flipped to `includes('#@')` (block now contains the verbatim marker).
  - L2428-2430 (Case 26, bare-@): `hasBlock(r, 'Refs api.md here.')` + `!hasBlock(r, 'Refs @api.md here.')` -> flip: the `@api.md` marker is now PRESENT verbatim. Also the +2 bug guard `!hasBlock(r, 'Refs pi.md here.')` may need review.
  - L1991-1992 (EDG-3, dedup): `hasBlock(r, 'Imports: specdoc and #@specdoc.md')` — the FIRST deduped marker was stripped; now it stays verbatim. Adjust to reflect both markers present.
2. INPUT: The updated engine from P1.M1.T1.S2 (injectMarkdown emits verbatim content). The current test assertions at the line numbers above.
3. LOGIC: For EACH of the ~13 pairs:
  - The POSITIVE assertion (stripped form present, e.g. `hasBlock(r, 'Imports api.md here.')`): this may still be TRUE if the path text appears independently, but the INTENT was to prove the marker was removed. Change it to assert the VERBATIM form instead: `hasBlock(r, 'Imports #@api.md here.')`.
  - The NEGATIVE assertion (marker form absent, e.g. `!hasBlock(r, 'Imports #@api.md here.')`): FLIP to POSITIVE — the marker is now PRESENT: `hasBlock(r, 'Imports #@api.md here.')`.
  - For bare-@ markers (Case 26): the `@api.md` marker (prefixLen 1) is now present verbatim instead of being stripped to `api.md`.
  - For the `!includes('#@')` blanket check (Case 20, L1753): REMOVE it or flip to `includes('#@')` — the block now contains verbatim markers.
  - For dedup cases (EDG-3): the deduped marker that was previously stripped now stays verbatim. Both the deduped (kept verbatim) and the dedup-winner (also verbatim) are present.
  - Update assertion comments from 'stripped' to 'verbatim' / 'marker preserved'.
  - IMPORTANT: the verbatim-block assertions (markers that were ALWAYS left verbatim — Case 16 fenced code, Case 17 cycle, Case 18 absolute, MD1/EDG-1/EDG-2 missing imports, E5 unreadable) are ALREADY correct and need NO change. Only the STRIPPED pairs flip.
4. OUTPUT: All ~13 markdown block content pairs now assert markers are PRESENT verbatim. No assertion claims a marker was stripped.
5. DOCS: none — test file.

subtasks.title: Migrate partial-strip + verify verbatim-prompt assertions (~19 cases)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_assertions_analysis.md for exact line numbers. Two groups remain:
  (a) PARTIAL-STRIP assertions (FS1/FS2/FS3/F2 at L899-L933): these test that in a prompt with TWO #@ tokens, only the one that ACTUALLY injected had its #@ stripped, while the failed/directory/deduped one kept #@. After verbatim delivery, BOTH tokens keep #@ — the partial-strip asymmetry no longer exists.
    - L899 (F2): `r.text.startsWith('<!--#@file-injected--> Review a.ts')` -> must become `r.text.startsWith('<!--#@file-injected--> Review #@a.ts')` (both verbatim).
    - L915-916 (FS1): `r.text.startsWith('Review a.ts')` + `r.text.includes('#@missing.ts') === true` -> the startsWith changes to `r.text.startsWith('Review #@a.ts')` (both verbatim). The includes('#@missing.ts') stays true (was already verbatim).
    - L924-925 (FS2): same pattern — `r.text.startsWith('Review a.ts')` -> `r.text.startsWith('Review #@a.ts')`; `includes('#@src/')` stays true.
    - L933 (FS3): `r.text.startsWith('Compare a.ts with #@a.ts')` -> must become `r.text.startsWith('Compare #@a.ts with #@a.ts')` (BOTH verbatim now — the first was stripped before, now it keeps #@; the deduped second was already verbatim).
  (b) VERBATIM-PROMPT assertions (~13 cases at L481, L489, L496, L503, L588, L628, L643, L988, L992, L1005, L1932, L2013, L2416): these already assert `r.text === '<original prompt with #@>'` — they are ALREADY CORRECT and need NO change. Verify they still pass (they should, since nothing injected means the prompt was always verbatim).
2. INPUT: The updated engine from P1.M1.T1/P1.M1.T2. The current test assertions at the line numbers above.
3. LOGIC:
  - For partial-strip cases (FS1/FS2/FS3/F2): change every `startsWith('... a.ts')` to include the #@ marker: `startsWith('... #@a.ts')`. The key insight is that ALL #@ markers now survive — there is no longer a distinction between 'injected marker stripped' and 'failed marker kept'. Every marker is verbatim.
  - For FS3 specifically: the prompt was `'Compare #@a.ts with #@a.ts'` (two identical tokens). Previously the first injected (stripped to `a.ts`) and the second deduped (kept `#@a.ts`). Now BOTH stay `#@a.ts`. Change `startsWith('Compare a.ts with #@a.ts')` to `startsWith('Compare #@a.ts with #@a.ts')` or to exact equality `r.text === 'Compare #@a.ts with #@a.ts'`.
  - For verbatim-prompt cases: VERIFY they pass unchanged. If any fail, it means the engine regressed (a prompt with no #@ or only failed tokens was modified — it shouldn't be).
  - Update assertion comments: remove 'first stripped, second keeps #@' language; all markers are now verbatim.
4. OUTPUT: All partial-strip and verbatim-prompt assertions in file-injector.test.mjs are consistent with verbatim delivery. `node file-injector.test.mjs` passes.
5. DOCS: none — test file.

tasks.title: Migrate marker assertions in relative-imports.test.mjs and import-behavior.test.mjs
tasks.description: These two focused test suites check import resolution correctness (file-relative vs cwd-relative). They search joined block content via has()/blocksText() for markers. Most assertions check content markers like 'B-FILE-RELATIVE' to prove the correct file was injected — these are unchanged. A few check marker presence/stripping and must be updated for verbatim delivery.

subtasks.title: Update marker-presence expectations in relative-imports.test.mjs and import-behavior.test.mjs
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_assertions_analysis.md. These suites primarily test import RESOLUTION (which file gets injected), not stripping. Most assertions use content markers (e.g. 'B-FILE-RELATIVE', 'B-MARKER') that are unaffected by verbatim delivery. The marker-specific assertions are:
  relative-imports.test.mjs:
  - C9 (L344): `has(blocksText(out), '#@ghost.md')` — asserts a MISSING import marker is left verbatim. This is ALREADY CORRECT (missing imports were always verbatim) — no change needed. Verify it still passes.
  - Other cases (C1-C8, C10-C12, D1-D7, E1-E3): assert on content markers, not on #@ presence/stripping. These are UNCHANGED. However, some may have implicit assumptions that resolved markers were stripped from the parent block — if any assertion checks that a resolved import marker is ABSENT from the parent's block content, it must flip to PRESENT.
  import-behavior.test.mjs:
  - 4f (L194): `has(o, '@weird.md.bak')` — extended token left verbatim (ALREADY CORRECT, missing file). Verify.
  - 4h (L205): `has(o, '@api.md.old')` — same pattern (ALREADY CORRECT). Verify.
  - 2c/5f: `!has(o, 'B-MARKER')` / `!has(o, 'C-MARKER')` — with markdownBareAtImports OFF, bare @ is inert (content absent). These test NON-INJECTION, not stripping — UNCHANGED.
2. INPUT: The updated engine from P1.M1.T1/P1.M1.T2. The current test files.
3. LOGIC:
  a) Run both test files after M1 changes: `node relative-imports.test.mjs` and `node import-behavior.test.mjs`. Identify any FAILURES.
  b) For any failure caused by a marker that was previously stripped but is now verbatim: flip the assertion from 'marker absent' to 'marker present'. The pattern is: `!has(text, '#@import.md')` -> `has(text, '#@import.md')` (or remove the negative assertion if it was checking stripping).
  c) For assertions about content markers ('B-FILE-RELATIVE' etc.) that prove the CORRECT FILE was injected: these should still pass — the file is still injected, just with its marker preserved in the parent's block. No change needed unless the test was implicitly relying on the marker being stripped.
  d) Update any comments that say 'stripped' or 'marker removed' to 'verbatim' / 'marker preserved'.
  e) MOST LIKELY OUTCOME: these two suites have very few stripping-dependent assertions (the analysis found only 1 in relative-imports and 2 in import-behavior, all of which already assert VERBATIM behavior for missing/unresolved tokens). The primary risk is implicit assumptions in content-marker assertions. Run and fix failures.
4. OUTPUT: Both `node relative-imports.test.mjs` and `node import-behavior.test.mjs` pass with verbatim delivery. `npm test` (all three suites chained) is green.
5. DOCS: none — test files.

tasks.title: Add re-open / re-submit regression tests (PRD §11 case #42 + §10 new rows)
tasks.description: The core value of verbatim delivery is that cancel/fork/tree-navigate re-submission re-triggers injection. No existing test covers this — it's a NEW regression guard. The test must model the Pi constraint: the stored user-message text is re-fed to a fresh input invocation, and injection must re-trigger because #@ survived in the stored text.

subtasks.title: Add return-shape, handler-level, and re-open simulation regression tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The test harness uses two invocation modes (see architecture/test_assertions_analysis.md §7): (a) direct pipeline call `mod.injectFiles(prompt, imagesIn, ctx, bareAt)` returning { text, images, injected, paged, blocks, details }, and (b) handler capture via `mod.default(pi)` + manual callback drive. The captureAllHandlers() helper captures input + before_agent_start sharing the SAME factory closure (the pending stash handoff). Tests load the extension via jiti from the global pi package.
2. INPUT: The test harness patterns from file-injector.test.mjs (makeMockCtx, captureAllHandlers, captureHandler). The completed verbatim engine from P1.M1.
3. LOGIC: Add new test cases to file-injector.test.mjs covering:
  (a) RETURN SHAPE — `injectFiles('Review #@a.ts', [], ctx, false)` returns `text === 'Review #@a.ts'` (verbatim, #@ preserved) AND `blocks.length === 1` AND `details.length === 1` AND `injected === 1`. This pins the verbatim contract at the pipeline level.
  (b) HANDLER-LEVEL — capture the input handler, invoke with `{ text: 'Review #@a.ts', source: 'interactive', images: [] }`, assert `out.text === 'Review #@a.ts'` (verbatim) and `out.action === 'transform'`. Then capture before_agent_start, invoke, assert the returned `msg.message.content` contains `<file name=` (the block was delivered).
  (c) RE-OPEN SIMULATION (the KEY regression) — model the Pi constraint:
     1. First submission: `const out1 = await h.input[0]({ text: 'Review #@a.ts', source: 'interactive', images: [] }, ctx)`. Assert `out1.text === 'Review #@a.ts'` (verbatim — this is what gets STORED).
     2. Simulate re-open: Pi re-feeds the STORED text (out1.text) to a FRESH input invocation. Create a new factory closure (or reuse): `const out2 = await h2.input[0]({ text: out1.text, source: 'interactive', images: [] }, ctx2)`. Assert `out2.action === 'transform'` (injection re-triggered!) and `out2.text === 'Review #@a.ts'` (still verbatim). This proves #@ survived in the stored text and re-triggered injection.
     3. If #@ had been stripped (out1.text === 'Review a.ts'), the re-open would find no #@ and return { action: 'continue' } — files would vanish. This test catches that regression.
  (d) Use the existing makeMockCtx/captureAllHandlers helpers. Create temp files (a.ts) in TMPDIR as other tests do.
4. OUTPUT: 3-4 new test cases in file-injector.test.mjs that pin the verbatim re-submission contract. All pass against the verbatim engine.
5. DOCS: none — test file.

subtasks.title: Add markdown verbatim regression test (import markers preserved in delivered content)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: After P1.M1.T1.S2, injectMarkdown delivers the markdown file's content VERBATIM — import markers (#@api.md) are preserved in the <file> block. Previously they were stripped. The existing Case 15 test (L1634+) was migrated in P1.M2.T1.S2 to assert the marker is present. This subtask adds an EXPLICIT regression test that models the full chain: top-level #@notes.md -> notes.md contains #@api.md -> api.md is injected -> notes.md's block contains the literal '#@api.md' marker.
2. INPUT: The migrated test assertions from P1.M2.T1.S2. The completed verbatim engine from P1.M1.
3. LOGIC: Add a new test case to file-injector.test.mjs:
  - Setup: create notes.md containing the text 'See #@api.md for details' and create api.md with some content. Both in TMPDIR.
  - Call: `const r = await mod.injectFiles('Review #@notes.md', [], ctx, false)` where ctx.cwd = TMPDIR.
  - Assert: `r.text === 'Review #@notes.md'` (top-level marker verbatim). `r.blocks.length === 2` (notes.md + api.md). `hasBlock(r, 'See #@api.md for details')` (the import marker is preserved verbatim in notes.md's block content — NOT stripped to 'See api.md for details'). `hasBlock(r, '<api.md content>')` (api.md was still injected despite the marker being preserved).
  - Also test with markdownBareAtImports ON: notes.md containing '@api.md' (bare @). Assert the '@api.md' marker is preserved verbatim in the block.
4. OUTPUT: 1-2 new test cases pinning that markdown import markers survive verbatim in delivered content while imports still resolve and inject. All pass.
5. DOCS: none — test file.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (README.md)
tasks.description: Mode B changeset-level documentation sync. The README currently describes stripping behavior (lines 41, 43, 72) that no longer matches the implementation. This task updates those passages to describe verbatim delivery: #@ triggers stay in the message as typed, so cancel/fork/re-open re-trigger injection. This task depends on ALL implementing subtasks (P1.M1 + P1.M2.T1/T2/T3) because it summarizes the complete behavioral delta.

subtasks.title: Update README.md lines 41, 43, 72 for verbatim prompt behavior
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/readme_analysis.md for exact text. Three passages need updating:
  - L41 (Usage section): currently says 'The #@ trigger is stripped from each reference, so Review #@a.ts appears in your message as Review a.ts, with the file delivered to the model underneath — never pasted into your message bubble.'
  - L43 (Usage section): currently says 'Markdown files can import other files. If spec.md itself contains #@api.md, a single #@spec.md delivers both — spec.md first, then api.md — and the import marker is stripped from spec.md the same way a top-level marker is:'
  - L72 (What gets injected section): currently says 'Your own message shows only what you typed.' — this needs review for consistency with verbatim delivery (it's actually still correct in spirit, but may need a clarifying note that #@ markers are preserved).
  - Lines 60, 88, 94, 95, 96, 135 mention 'left as written' / 'left verbatim' / 'left as-is' for NON-matching tokens — these are CORRECT and must NOT be conflated with marker stripping. Do not touch them.
2. INPUT: The completed verbatim engine and passing tests from P1.M1 + P1.M2.T1/T2/T3. The current README.md.
3. LOGIC:
  a) L41: Rewrite to: '#@ triggers stay in your message exactly as you typed them (Review #@a.ts stays Review #@a.ts), so cancelling and re-opening, forking, or re-submitting re-triggers injection. The file bytes are delivered to the model underneath — never pasted into your message bubble. Each file renders as a compact green read <path> line below your message.'
  b) L43: Rewrite to: 'Markdown files can import other files. If spec.md itself contains #@api.md, a single #@spec.md delivers both — spec.md first, then api.md. The import marker stays in spec.md verbatim (same as a top-level marker):'
  c) L72: Review 'Your own message shows only what you typed.' — this is still accurate (the user sees their prompt with #@ markers, not file contents). Optionally add a clarifying note: 'Your own message shows exactly what you typed — including the #@ markers — so re-opening or forking re-triggers injection automatically.'
  d) Do NOT touch: install instructions, syntax grammar, config (markdownBareAtImports), limits, #@ vs @ section, file-type table, or the 'left as written' passages for non-matching tokens.
4. OUTPUT: README.md accurately describes verbatim delivery. No passage claims markers are stripped.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B). It updates README.md — the changeset-level overview — to reflect the verbatim prompt behavior. All implementing subtasks (P1.M1 + P1.M2.T1/T2/T3) must be complete before this runs.
